Title: RM150mil allocation for Mitra a historic moment for Indian community - Ramanan
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The boost in the allocation for the Malaysian Indian Community Transformation Unit (Mitra) to RM150 million, from RM100 million previously, marks a historic moment for the Indian community in the country.
Human Resources Minister Datuk Seri R. Ramanan said the additional RM50 million announced by Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today is the first increase in funding for the agency since its establishment more than two decades ago.
"Thank you to the prime minister for the additional RM50 million. This is the first time since Mitra's establishment, even when it was known as SEDIC (Special Unit for the Socioeconomic Development of the Indian Community), that the allocation has been increased.
"For over 20 years, it remained at around RM100 million," he said after attending a meet-and-greet session between the prime minister and Indian students at Universiti Malaya here today.
Mitra, which was previously under the Prime Minister's Department, is now under the Human Resources Ministry.
Formerly known as SEDIC, Mitra is a special unit tasked with addressing the socioeconomic development of the Indian community in Malaysia.
Ramanan said he deeply appreciates the Madani government's commitment to empowering the Indian community through various socioeconomic development initiatives.
He said assistance for the Indian community is not limited to Mitra allocations, it also includes a range of programmes under other ministries and government agencies.
"There are many initiatives. Even before, there were programmes under Amanah Ikhtiar Malaysia (AIM), where RM100 million was allocated for the Prosperity Empowerment and A New Normal for Indian Women (PENN 2.0) programme.
"Tekun Nasional has also allocated RM100 million specifically for the Indian community, while SME Bank has set aside RM50 million for Indian entrepreneurs," he said.
Earlier in his speech, Ramanan said Mitra is entering a new, more strategic phase by focusing on education development as the core of human capital development for the Indian community starting next year.
"Under this new direction, Mitra will focus on comprehensive education development, from preschool, primary, secondary and higher education, including advanced technical training," he said.
He added that Mitra will also explore strategic collaborations, including overseas technical training, to enhance the competitiveness and employability of Indian youth at the national level.
– Bernama
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Shooting incident: Tan Lek Khang regains consciousness, still weak
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Former Johor Bersatu associate chief Datuk Tan Lek Khang, who was seriously injured after allegedly shooting himself in the chest on April 28, has regained consciousness.
His friend and former party information chief, Peter Khoo Kong Ek, confirmed that Tan was still undergoing treatment at a hospital here and might require further follow-up surgery.
"He has regained consciousness, but is still weak although he is able to speak a little," he told Bernama today.
Tan, a businessman, had been admitted to the intensive care unit (ICU) at Sultan Ismail Hospital following a four-hour operation to remove a bullet from his chest.
Johor police chief Datuk Ab Rahaman Arsad was previously reported as saying that Tan was believed to have
accidentally discharged
his firearm while unloading it after a shooting practice session at the Ladang Rem Shooting Range in Kota Tinggi.
Tan is said to have held a valid firearms licence since 2022 for personal security and sports shooting purposes.
Police have recorded statements from
nine witnesses
to assist investigations, including five bodyguards, Tan's 19-year-old stepson, a shooting instructor and two of his friends.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Iran army says countries siding with US to 'face difficulties' in Hormuz
Article:
TEHRAN: Iran's army warned on Sunday that countries complying with US sanctions against the Islamic republic would face difficulties crossing the Strait of Hormuz.
Early this month the US government imposed new sanctions on Iranian interests and warned ships against paying authorities in Tehran to pass through the strait.
The United States and Bahrain have drafted a UN resolution, seen by AFP, calling on Iran to halt restrictions on shipping through the waterway, which has become a major flashpoint since the outbreak of war on February 28.
"Countries that comply with the United States by imposing sanctions on the Islamic Republic of Iran will certainly face difficulties crossing the strait," army official Mohammad Akraminia told Iran's official IRNA news agency on Sunday.
"We have established a new legal and security system in the Strait of Hormuz. From now on, any vessel wishing to pass through it must coordinate with us," he added.
He said the system was "now in force" and would bring "economic, security and political gains."
On Saturday, Ebrahim Azizi, head of the Iranian parliament's national security commission, issued a similar warning in a post on X.
"We warn governments, including microstates like Bahrain, that siding with the US-backed resolution will bring severe consequences. The Strait of Hormuz is a vital lifeline; do not risk closing it on yourselves forever," he wrote.
Iran has allowed only a trickle of ships to pass through the waterway, a route that in peacetime accounts for a fifth of the world's oil and gas flows, along with other vital commodities.
Last month, Iranian deputy speaker of parliament Hamidreza Hajibabaei said Tehran had received its first revenue from the tolls it imposed on the strait.
Veto-wielding Russia has warned it is prepared to block the UN Security Council resolution proposed by the United States and Bahrain, according to diplomatic sources.  — AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: No Bersatu outcast applied to join Pas despite remaining pro-PN
Article:
KOTA BARU: None of the elected representatives or leaders sacked or suspended by Bersatu has sought to join Pas despite remaining within Perikatan Nasional (PN).
Pas secretary-general Datuk Seri Takiyuddin Hassan said the party had not received any application or request from the Bersatu outcasts.
Among them is Tanah Merah MP Datuk Seri Ikmal Hisham Abdul Aziz, who has ruled out appealing against his suspension over alleged disciplinary breaches.
"To us, this (the suspension and sacking of Bersatu leaders) is a Bersatu issue," Takiyuddin said when contacted, declining further comment.
Aside from Ikmal, four other MPs - Datuk Khlir Mohd Nor (Ketereh), Datuk Abdul Khalib Abdullah (Rompin), Nordin Ahmad Ismail (Lumut) and Datuk Rosol Wahid (Hulu Terengganu) - were also suspended.
Their suspensions were announced by the party's disciplinary board, which also confirmed the dismissals of Kedah assemblymen Dzowahir Ab Ghani (Suka Menanti) and Abdul Razak Khamis (Sungai Tiang).
Ikmal has said he believes his suspension was
linked to his ties
with Datuk Seri Hamzah Zainudin, the former Bersatu deputy president who was
sacked
by the party in February.
The sacking came amid tensions between Bersatu and Pas in Perlis, which eventually saw Bersatu take over the state administration from Pas.
Speculation has been rife that Hamzah may announce the formation of a new party within PN, which currently comprises Bersatu, Pas, Gerakan and the Malaysian Indian People's Party (MIPP).
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Anwar calls on youth to reject racial politics, embrace unity
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim wants the younger generation to reject narrow racial sentiments, saying they must play a central role in shaping a more harmonious and stable Malaysia.
He said young Malaysians should emerge as a unifying force that transcends race and religion, rather than inheriting divisive attitudes from previous generations.
"Looking at the faces of the young ones, the boys and girls, the students, I see great hope. You do not necessarily represent the archaic racist sentiments of many past generations. This country cannot continue to tolerate these excesses," he said at a dialogue session with Indian students at Universiti Malaya.
Anwar, however, acknowledged that fostering such change would require time and patience.
"Yes, it takes time and patience. If I say something that benefits the Indian community, there will be factions of extremist Malays who will attack me endlessly on social media," he said.
"This is why I am appealing to you, especially the students. What is education after all? It is to acquire knowledge, disseminate truth, and dispense justice, as well as to engage in intellectual debate and discourse. Not to sow hatred, enmity and discontent."
He said education should encourage rational reflection and meaningful discussion, adding: "I am hopeful and optimistic."
Anwar also said that in several Western countries, far-right movements were gaining traction by exploiting racial sentiment and division.
He cautioned against similar trends emerging locally, saying Malaysia must remain mindful of its multicultural fabric.
He said leaders often faced criticism regardless of their decisions, whether involving issues affecting the Chinese, Indian or Malay communities, including matters such as temple developments or farming concerns.
"Even when we need to be firm, we must respect other people's culture, beliefs and religion while abiding by the rules. But we must not go so far as to incite resentment or hurt the sentiments of those carrying out their responsibilities," he said.
Anwar said that governing a diverse nation was complex, and that he remained open to correction and constructive feedback.
"I know I have a responsibility in leading this country, and the complexities are not easy. As always, I stand to be corrected," he said.
He also said that while the Malay community, as the majority, must respect the rights of others, minority groups too must recognise the importance of social harmony.
"The Chinese and Indians can make their demands, but they must also remember that this nation should not allow strong racist reactions that could destroy the fabric of our society," he said.
Anwar expressed confidence that Malaysia would continue to uphold the Madani framework in safeguarding the welfare, interests and rights of all citizens.
"I am optimistic. Insya-Allah, this country will continue to embrace the Madani framework for every single citizen," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: New Zealand pedestrian injured in Chow Kit motorcycle crash
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A 49-year-old New Zealand pedestrian was injured after being hit by a motorcycle at Jalan Chow Kit early this morning.
City police Traffic Enforcement and Investigation Department (TEID) chief Assistant Commissioner Mohd Zamzuri Mohd Isa said the department received a report on the incident, which occurred at about 2.37am.
"Preliminary investigations found that the accident involved a Yamaha Y15ZR motorcycle ridden by a 26-year-old local man and a 49-year-old male pedestrian from New Zealand.
"The accident is believed to have occurred when the pedestrian suddenly crossed the road at a location not designated as a pedestrian crossing, resulting in a collision.
"The motorcyclist suffered minor injuries to his hands and legs, while the pedestrian sustained a fractured right leg, injuries to the back of his head and swelling on his right hand. He is currently receiving treatment at Kuala Lumpur Hospital," he said.
He said both have been identified, with the motorcyclist, who sustained minor injuries, called in to give his statement.
"The TEID has also carried out further actions, including reviewing closed-circuit television (CCTV) footage, inspecting the scene and recording statements from those involved," he said.
He said the case is being investigated under Section 43(1) of the Road Transport Act 1987 for driving without due care and attention or without reasonable consideration for other road users.
If found guilty, the offence carries a fine of RM5,000 to RM10,000 and up to 12 months' jail.
Previously, a video of the incident went viral, showing several ambulances at the scene while motorists continued passing by.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Proposed ombudsman to boost public confidence in complaints process - Azalina
Article:
BANGI: The proposed Malaysian Ombudsman is aimed at addressing public perceptions over the transparency of internal complaint processes and does not mean existing mechanisms are ineffective.
Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Law and Institutional Reform) Datuk Seri Azalina Othman Said said the Public Complaints Bureau (BPA) is not ineffective but it is subject to public perceptions concerning the transparency and outcomes of complaints because it operates within the government's administrative structure.
"I'm not saying it (BPA) is ineffective. The problem is when the mechanism is internal, sometimes many people question its outcome. People may even think twice before lodging a report.
"The ombudsman will separate the Public Complaints Bureau from government agencies. It will be an independent agency. An independent body," she said at a press conference after launching the Madani Accountability Framework 2026 Town Hall programme here today.
She said the move is important to avoid public hesitation in lodging complaints, while ensuring every report of misconduct is examined seriously and without bias.
"So my point is that the whole purpose of the ombudsman is to separate it from government institutions so that the people will have confidence and complaints will be taken seriously.
"We are in government service; we are not perfect all the time," she said.
Earlier, during a question-and-answer session with town hall participants, Azalina said the ombudsman would not be an enforcement body, but would instead receive complaints, conduct investigations and make recommendations to the disciplinary board of the relevant agency for further action.
She said the recommendations would also be tabled in Parliament as part of the institutional checks and balances process.
"For example, like the IPCC (Independent Police Conduct Commission), they have their own mini ombudsman. So they do their own investigation.
"The same thing for the MACC (Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission).
"So these are all things that we have to sort out before we finalise how we want the ombudsman to be implemented. I have always believed that in our country, the best way to do things is step by step," she said.
On the drafting of the Freedom of Information (FOI) Act, Azalina said it would be among the key reforms in strengthening the culture of government openness and ensuring the public's right to access information is better protected.
She said the effort is in line with current needs to improve transparency in public administration, particularly in the dissemination of information to the people.
"Can you imagine if you write a letter to a government department, but it goes unanswered for months? This is why the FOI is needed, because questions must be answered," she said.
Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim was reported as saying that the proposal to establish the
Malaysian Ombudsman
and the drafting of the FOI Act were undergoing engagement sessions and were expected to be tabled during the 2026 Parliament sitting.
The media recently reported Azalina as saying that both institutional reform initiatives involving the government would be tabled at the upcoming Parliament sitting.
According to the Parliament portal, the Second Meeting of the Fifth Session of the 15th Parliament for the Dewan Rakyat sitting will be held for 16 days from June 22 to July 16 this year.
– Bernama
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Lorry in fatal crash probe catches fire outside Kuala Pilah police HQ
Article:
KUALA PILAH: A lorry involved in a fatal accident investigation caught fire outside the Kuala Pilah police headquarters early this morning.
District police chief Superintendent Muhamad Mustafah Hussin said police were alerted to the fire at 2.45am.
He said firefighters arrived at the scene at 3.05am and extinguished the blaze within 20 minutes.
Mustafah said the lorry was involved in an accident being investigated under Section 41(1) of the Road Transport Act for causing death by reckless or dangerous driving.
"The accident involved the lorry and two cars, and resulted in one death," he said.
He said while the two cars were kept inside the police headquarters compound, the lorry had to be parked outside due to space constraints.
Mustafah said the cause of the fire has yet to be determined and investigations are ongoing.
"The Fire and Rescue Department's forensic team will investigate and determine the cause of the fire," he said.
He urged anyone with information on the incident to contact the Kuala Pilah police headquarters at 06-4842999 or investigating officer Inspector Supiah Ishak at 019-9933978.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Ministers extend Mother's Day wishes, honour sacrifices of mothers
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Cabinet members, including Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Fadillah Yusof, today shared Mother's Day greetings and messages of appreciation through their respective social media platforms to honour the sacrifices, love and dedication of mothers.
Fadillah described mothers as extraordinary women who serve as the backbone of families, children's first educators and an endless source of love.
He said the role of mothers in managing households has become increasingly challenging amid changing economic and social conditions, as well as the demands of raising children in the era of technology and social media.
In facing these challenges, he said the Madani Government continues to strengthen various initiatives to ensure supply stability and reduce the burden on the people, particularly families and mothers who play a central role in household management.
"The strength of a nation begins with a strong family institution, and at the heart of that strength stands a mother who is resilient, patient and full of love.
"Happy Mother's Day. May every sacrifice and expression of love always be blessed, appreciated and become an inspiration to children and future generations," he said in a Facebook post.
Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil also extended his Mother's Day greetings, expressing appreciation for the love, sacrifices, and unwavering prayers of mothers.
"May all mothers be blessed with good health, happiness, and continued strength," he said.
Education Minister Fadhlina Sidek described mothers as special individuals and heroes of love who bravely and steadfastly endure life's challenges for the sake of their children and families.
"A mother's sincere love never changes. Her sacrifices and contributions can never be repaid. Nothing is stronger than a mother's love. Thank you to all the great women who proudly carry the title of mother," she said in a Facebook post.
Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Minister Datuk Armizan Mohd Ali said a mother's sacrifices are reflected through her endless prayers, unconditional love and willingness to become a pillar of strength in all situations.
"In conjunction with Mother's Day, let us honour and appreciate those who have sacrificed so much to see their children happy and successful.
While there is still time, hug your mother, cherish her and pray for her every day," he said.
Human Resources Minister Datuk Seri R. Ramanan also extended Mother's Day greetings to all mothers and women who tirelessly provide unconditional love, strength and sacrifice.
"May every sacrifice you make always be appreciated and blessed," he said.
Meanwhile, Agriculture and Food Security Minister Datuk Seri Mohamad Sabu expressed special appreciation to mothers working as farmers, livestock rearers, fishermen, and agro-food workers across the country.
"Early in the morning, they head to the rice fields, the sea, and plantations to earn a living and ensure food is always on Malaysians' tables. Their sacrifices are immense and deeply meaningful to the nation.
"Thank you to all mothers who never tire of sacrificing for their families and communities. A mother's love and devotion are beyond measure and cannot be repaid with anything," he said.
In a similar message, Works Minister Datuk Seri Alexander Nanta Linggi reminded the public not to forget the role of mothers behind every achievement and moment of success.
"To those who have lost their mothers, may they rest in peace. To those who still have their mothers, cherish them and continue to be blessed by their love," he said.
Mother's Day is celebrated worldwide on the second Sunday of May each year.
– Bernama
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Health Ministry launches Lung Shield programme to screen 10,000 traffic cops for lung cancer
Article:
CYBERJAYA: The Health Ministry has launched a large-scale Lung Shield programme aimed at screening 10,000 traffic police personnel over the next three years in a bid to detect lung cancer at its earliest stages through advanced AI-assisted technology.
Officiating the launch during the 2026 World No Tobacco Day celebration, Health Minister Datuk Seri Dr Dzulkefly Ahmad described the initiative as a key milestone in Malaysia's journey towards a smoke-free future.
He said the first cohort would focus on traffic police personnel from the Traffic Investigation and Enforcement Department, who are frequently exposed to environmental hazards in the course of their duties.
"This is a very appropriate choice for the first target cohort as they are not only potentially linked to cigarette use but are also exposed to 'chemical insults' or lung stress in the course of their duties," he said.
The programme will employ advanced diagnostic tools, including artificial intelligence (AI)-assisted chest X-rays and low-dose CT scans, to identify lung abnormalities at the earliest possible stage.
Dzulkefly said that early detection remained critical, adding that survival rates for Stage 1 lung cancer stood at about 37 per cent, compared with just six per cent for cases detected at Stage 3 or 4.
"Every time we detect cancer, it is usually at a late stage, meaning the prognosis or ability to treat it is significantly lower," he said at a press conference after the launch.
The initiative also includes a corporate social responsibility (CSR) component by IHH Healthcare Malaysia, which provides micro-insurance coverage for participants under the programme.
Dzulkefly said that Lung Shield was not a pilot project but a fully operational initiative, with plans to scale it up through greater participation from corporate partners in the healthcare sector.
Beyond screening, he highlighted the enforcement of Act 852, which regulates all smoking products, including electronic cigarettes, under a single comprehensive legal framework.
He described tobacco as a "scourge of humanity", reiterating that it remained the only legal product that kills one in two of its users when consumed as intended.
He also urged the tobacco industry to act responsibly and avoid marketing nicotine products in ways that could appeal to minors.
Under Act 852, the government has already imposed strict bans on advertising, promotion and public display of smoking products as part of efforts to protect younger generations from nicotine addiction.
Findings from the Lung Shield programme are expected to be presented by Malaysia at the 79th World Health Assembly in Geneva in May 2026.
"We want to move further with the involvement of more strategic partners from the healthcare industry to strengthen our national lung health agenda," Dzulkefly said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Vijay becomes Tamil Nadu's new chief minister
Article:
HYDERABAD: Film star-turned-politician C. Joseph Vijay was sworn in as Tamil Nadu state's new chief minister on Sunday after he was promised support by other parties to achieve the required legislative majority.
His Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam (TVK) party supporters celebrated by bursting crackers and distributing sweets in various cities as the 51-year-old was administered the oath of office by the state governor at Chennai's Jawaharlal Nehru Stadium.
His father S.A. Chandrasekhar, mother Shobha, Tamil actress Trisha Krishnan and Congress party leader Rahul Gandhi were present.
Nine cabinet ministers also took oath on the occasion.
Vijay promised a transparent government and better law and order in the state of 77 million. The TVK won 108 seats in April's assembly election, falling short of the 118 needed for a majority of its own in the 234-member chamber.
[[nid:1436484]]
The Congress, a key ally of the former ruling party Dravida Munnetra Kazhagam (DMK), extended support to Vijay along with Communist Party of India (CPI), Communist Party of India-Marxist (CPI-M), Viduthalai Chiruthaigal Katchi (VCK), and the Indian Union Muslim League (IUML) to end the uncertainty over the new government.
State governor Rajendra Vishwanath Arlekar on Saturday appointed Vijay as chief minister and asked him to seek a vote of confidence on or before May 13.
DMK spokesman Saravanan Annadurai earlier said former chief minister M.K. Stalin did not want a constitutional crisis in the state and persuaded his allies to extend support to Vijay in government formation, according to local media reports.
The Congress has five seats and the other four parties have two members each in the newly-elected assembly. – BERNAMA
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Anwar extends Mother's Day wishes, expresses appreciation
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today extended Mother's Day greetings to all mothers, expressing appreciation for their sacrifices, love and unwavering devotion.
In a post on his Facebook page, Anwar shared a series of poetic verses or "pantun" dedicated to honouring the role and contributions of mothers.
"Burung melayang di puncak angkasa,
Hinggap sebentar di pohon jati,
Kasih ibu berkalung jasa,
Menjadi azimat terangi hati.
"Happy Mother's Day!" he said.
The prime minister also uploaded a photograph of his late mother, Che Yan Hamid Hussein, alongside his wife, Datuk Seri Dr Wan Azizah Wan Ismail, as a tribute to two important women in his life.
Mother's Day is celebrated worldwide on the second Sunday of May each year.
This year's national -level Mother's Day celebration carries the theme "Seteguh Kasih Ibu".
– Bernama
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Healthcare drag clouds Pentamaster outlook
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Pentamaster Corp Bhd's earnings are expected to face near-term pressure from its newly-commercialised healthcare segment, according to Phillip Capital Sdn Bhd.
Despite a stronger revenue growth in the first quarter of financial year 2026 (Q1 FY26), the company's earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortisation (Ebitda) margin fell 4.6 percentage points.
The Ebitda was weighed down by higher fixed costs associated with the healthcare segment's commercialisation phase.
Pentamaster's revenue rose 37 per cent to RM180 million, driven by a strong recovery in the factory automation solutions segment, supported by robust demand from medical and electro-optical applications.
However, growth was partially offset by a nine per cent decline in the automated test equipment segment, reflecting softer demand in automotive and consumer and industrial markets.
"Stripping out RM5 million foreign exchange gain and RM2 million forward exchange contract loss, Q1 FY26 core net profit of RM15 million was below ours and consensus expectations at 16 per cent of both respective forecasts.
"The earnings shortfall was due to larger-than-expected losses from the newly commercialised healthcare segment," it said.
Phillip Capital said the segment is still in early-stage commercialisation and is likely to remain loss-making through 2026, continuing to weigh on overall earnings.
Despite near-term pressure, Pentamaster's order book improved to RM480 million in Q1 FY26, up 20 per cent quarter-on-quarter from RM400 million at end-2025.
The firm said outstanding order book was supported by medical (55 per cent) and consumer and industrial products (24 per cent), followed by automotive (11 per cent), opto-electronics (6.0 per cent) and others (5 per cent).
"We continue to see the medical, electro-optical and automotive segment as the group's main growth driver," it said.
Phillip Capital cut its FY26-FY27 earnings forecasts on Pentamaster between eight per cent and 24 per cent, reflecting deeper-than-expected losses from the healthcare segment.
The firm said this structural shift in earnings quality has led it to downgrade the stock to "Hold" from "Buy", although it raised its 12-month target price to RM4.10 from RM3.52.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Police await MCMC report in probe over alleged insult to Sultan of Selangor
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: Police are awaiting a forensic analysis report from the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) before referring the investigation paper involving a man accused of insulting the Sultan of Selangor on social media to the Attorney-General's Chambers (A-GC).
Deputy Inspector-General of Police Tan Sri Ayob Khan Mydin Pitchay said the man was arrested on May 3 and remanded for three days until May 6 to assist investigations.
"The case involves insults against the Sultan of Selangor. We have sent the seized items to MCMC for forensic data analysis, and this may take some time.
"We have obtained the investigation data and are now waiting for the forensic analysis and data extraction reports. Once we receive them, the investigation paper will be referred again to the AGC for further instructions," he told reporters here today.
He was met after the crime prevention with community carnival and launch of Taman Angkat Amanita of the state police contingent here today.
Ayob said the man had been released after his remand period ended, pending completion of the investigation.
"If we receive instructions to charge him after the investigation paper is completed, we will proceed. It is not a case of arresting someone today and charging them the next day.
"We need to ensure the data gathered and analysed by MCMC is complete so that the investigation and any charges brought are thorough," he said.
He said police had also received another report linked to the case, lodged in Penang, and investigations were ongoing.
He said authorities were also probing other cases involving allegedly sensitive content uploaded on social media, including posts touching on royalty and religion.
"Regardless of a person's background, we do not compromise. Since I first joined the force until now, my stand has remained the same.
"Any individual, from any party, group or religion, who crosses the line of the law will face action. If there is a case, we will investigate and arrest," he said.
He said the increasing trend of social media users questioning sensitive matters could threaten harmony in the country if left unchecked.
"We have lived in harmony for more than 60 years. There are matters that can be discussed openly and there are matters that should be handled carefully.
"When sensitive issues involving race, religion and royalty are openly provoked, it can create tension in our multiracial society," he said.
Ayob urged the public to exercise restraint when commenting on sensitive matters online to avoid creating unrest and division.
On May 3, police detained a man in his 30s in Bukit Mertajam over a
social media post featuring images
allegedly insulting the Sultan of Selangor.
The arrest was carried out at 7.10pm by a team from the Bukit Aman Criminal Investigation Department in collaboration with Penang police.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Border urine tests net over 1,000 drug-positive travellers, says Nada
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The National Anti-Drugs Agency (Nada) detained 1,084 people who tested positive for drugs at Malaysia's border entry points between 2024 and March this year, with cases recorded mainly during festive periods and school holidays.
Nada director-general Datuk Ruslin Jusoh said many of those detained were returning Malaysians, including from Songkran celebrations in neighbouring countries, following preliminary urine screening at entry points in Kedah, Perlis and Kelantan.
"In 2024, 494 people tested positive, comprising 444 men and 50 women. Methamphetamine recorded the highest number of cases at 148, followed by ketamine (109), tetrahydrocannabinol (THC) or cannabis (96), MDMA (80), benzodiazepines (62), ketum (23) and opiates (four)," he said.
"For 2025, 415 people were detained, involving 392 men and 23 women. Methamphetamine again topped the list with 140 cases, followed by ketamine (108), THC (70), benzodiazepines (59), MDMA (31), opiates (five) and ketum (two).
"Up to March 31 this year, 175 people were detained, comprising 167 men and eight women. Methamphetamine recorded 65 cases, followed by ketamine (43), THC (32), MDMA (22) and ketum (13). All those detained were aged between 18 and their 60s," he said at the 2026 Drug-Free Aspiration Roadshow Programme in Kampung Kemensah, Ulu Klang.
Ruslin reminded Malaysians travelling abroad not to consume prohibited substances, warning that those who test positive upon return could face action under the Drug Dependants (Treatment and Rehabilitation) Act 1983.
"Upon arrival at the border, we conduct urine tests. Those who test positive can be detained under the Act for up to two years at rehabilitation centres," he said.
He added that Nada had deployed Border Anti-Drug Units at key entry points, including Padang Besar in Perlis, Bukit Kayu Hitam in Kedah, and Bukit Bunga, Pengkalan Kubor and Rantau Panjang in Kelantan, including ferry routes at Pengkalan Kubor, to monitor and inspect incoming travellers.
Ruslin said the campaign in Kampung Kemensah was also part of the Drug-Free Foster Village (KABD) initiative, a community empowerment programme aimed at transforming selected localities into drug-free zones through continuous monitoring, awareness and preventive efforts.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Miracle twins after 12-year wait: A Mother's Day story of hope and resilience
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A mother's 12-year wait for children was finally rewarded with the birth of twins following numerous fertility treatments, a journey for which she was honoured with the Special Mother's Inspiration Award during the National Mother's Day celebrations today.
Police officer Assistant Superintendent Nurul Zaanizila Mohd Zain, 44, said the long journey to motherhood is one filled with immense challenges.
She added that since 2008, she and her husband sought to conceive through various means, including Intrauterine Insemination (IUI) and In-Vitro Fertilisation (IVF).
"I tried various traditional and modern treatments. During that period, there were many failures. I had undergone IUI previously and my first IVF treatment was done elsewhere.
"I then underwent a second round of IVF with the National Population and Family Development Board, and I am grateful that it was successful," she said.
She added that she remained constantly cautious during her pregnancy, especially as her twins were eventually born prematurely.
"When I found out I was pregnant, I was on high alert throughout the entire term. I was terrified of any risks occurring, so I was constantly on guard. My prayers were non-stop.
"Alhamdulillah, even though they were premature and had to be placed in incubators at the time, my children survived. They are now six years old and very healthy," she said when met at the National Mother's Day 2026 Celebration at the World Trade Centre (WTC).
Zaanizila, who currently serves at the Shah Alam police headquarters, said she is grateful to God for her twins, Muhammad Aisy Noah and Nurul Raudhatul Aafiya.
Meanwhile, her husband, Mohd Azren Isa, said he had to remain steadfast to support his wife during those difficult years.
"Men do feel sadness, we just don't show it in front of our wives. If she is sad and I am sad too, she will feel even more discouraged.
"So, I tried to provide mental and physical support and fulfilled whatever she needed. Whenever we heard of a promising treatment, we tried it, but ultimately, our blessing came through the family development board," he said.
Recalling the moment he discovered they were expecting, Azren said he could not contain his emotions.
He said despite doctors warning them that a twin pregnancy carried high risks and required intensive care, their spirits remained unbroken.
"When the doctor said there was good news and bad news, my heart was racing.
"The doctor then told me the good news was that my wife was pregnant with twins. I broke down in tears because I was so grateful," he added.
[[nid:1436418]]
Zaanizila was named the recipient of the Special Mother's Inspiration Award by the Women, Family and Community Development Ministry today.
The award was presented by Raja Permaisuri of Perak, Tuanku Zara Salim, who graced the ceremony to officiate the celebration.
Also in attendance were Datuk Seri Dr Wan Azizah Wan Ismail, and Women, Family and Community Development Minister Datuk Seri Nancy Shukri.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Couple held over RM800,000 Taman Melawati break-in
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A married couple has been detained in connection with a house break-in at Taman Melawati that resulted in the loss of jewellery and watches worth about RM800,000.
Ampang Jaya district police chief sssistant commissioner Khairul Anuar Khalid said the Indonesian couple was arrested yesterday.
"At this time, we are unable to disclose further details regarding the arrests as investigations are still ongoing," he said in a statement.
It was reported that a house in
Taman Melawati
had been broken into, with losses estimated at RM800,000 involving jewellery and watches.
The incident was discovered when the homeowner returned at about 11.30pm and found the living room and several rooms in disarray.
Police investigations found that the suspects are believed to have entered the house through the back door by prying open the grille and door before ransacking several areas.
Police also found three fingerprint impressions at the scene to assist in investigations.
The case is being investigated under Section 457 of the Penal Code for housebreaking.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Tuanku Zara graces Mother's Day celebration, spotlight on support for mothers
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The National Mother's Day celebration opened on a royal note today as the Raja Permaisuri of Perak, Tuanku Zara Salim, graced the ceremony at the World Trade Centre (WTC), underscoring the nation's continued commitment to strengthening support for mothers and families.
Her Royal Highness was received upon arrival by the Prime Minister's wife, Datuk Seri Dr Wan Azizah Wan Ismail, and Women, Family and Community Development Minister Datuk Seri Nancy Shukri.
In her speech, Nancy said the government remained committed to reinforcing support systems for women and families through a comprehensive and inclusive approach centred on strengthening the family institution.
She said the ministry, through the National Population and Family Development Board, had been providing reproductive and fertility health services since 1979 to help couples realise their aspirations of parenthood.
"Through comprehensive fertility services at affordable rates, including treatment subsidies, we have managed more than 373,000 subfertility cases, with 6,513 successful pregnancies," she said.
The ceremony also highlighted personal stories of perseverance, including that of Nurul Zaanizila Mohd Zain, recipient of the Special Mother's Inspiration Award, who conceived after 12 years through in-vitro fertilisation (IVF) under the programme.
Nancy said the award was introduced to recognise women undergoing fertility treatment – often referred to as "two-line warriors" – and to inspire others facing similar struggles.
She said that the government had also introduced the Fertility Treatment Assistance and Subfertility Advocacy Programme, which provides funding for up to two cycles of intrauterine insemination (IUI) for eligible couples, benefiting an estimated 47,000 couples.
A national subfertility data registry with international standards will also be developed this year, as part of preparations for the proposed National Subfertility Centre in Shah Alam, expected to be operational by 2030.
Nancy said findings from the 2025 Mother's Day Public Opinion Survey showed that 65.9 per cent of mothers in Malaysia faced time constraints for themselves, particularly in terms of rest and emotional recovery.
In response, she said the ministry had introduced the Seteguh Kasih Ibu Package, comprising the Sayang Ibu, Jalinan Kasih Ibu and Minda Tenang initiatives, focusing on early cancer screening, mammogram subsidies, emotional support programmes and mental wellbeing assistance.
"This initiative reflects the government's commitment to a holistic support ecosystem so that mothers remain healthy, resilient and empowered in their role within the family," she said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: World deprived of 1 billion barrels of oil over past two months, Aramco CEO says
Article:
DUBAI: The world has lost about one billion barrels of oil over the past two months and energy markets will take time to stabilise even if flows resume, Saudi oil giant Saudi Aramco chief executive officer Amin Nasser said on Sunday, as shipping disruptions choke traffic through the Strait of Hormuz.
"Our objective is simple: keep energy flowing, even when the system is under strain," Nasser told Reuters in a statement after Aramco reported a 25 per cent jump in net profit in its first quarter.
Global energy supplies have been sharply squeezed by Iran's blockade of the Strait of Hormuz, which has curtailed shipping and driven prices higher following the US-Israeli war.
"Reopening routes is not the same as normalising a market that has been deprived of about one billion barrels of oil," Nasser said, adding that years of underinvestment have compounded the strain on already-low global inventories.
Aramco has used its East-West Pipeline to bypass Hormuz and transport crude to the Red Sea, an asset Nasser described as a "critical lifeline" to mitigate the global supply crisis.
Despite shifts in shipping routes, Nasser reiterated that Asia remained a key priority for the company and was central to global demand.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Some agencies push back against ombudsman plan, Azalina says
Article:
BANGI: Several government agencies are pushing back against the proposed ombudsman law, preferring to rely on their own internal monitoring mechanisms instead of an independent oversight body.
Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Law and Institutional Reform) Datuk Seri Azalina Othman Said said negotiations are ongoing to bring these agencies on board.
"We are still trying to get more negotiations done. As I mentioned, some agencies feel that they do not want to be under the ombudsman because they claim they have their own internal monitoring, so they do not want it.
"They are still in discussions," she said after the Madani Accountability Framework town hall here today.
She said the mechanism would also improve public perception of the complaints system, which was previously handled by the Public Complaints Bureau (BPA) under the Prime Minister's Department.
"You are supposed to complain about a government agency to a body that is independent. But previously, what we had was the BPA under the Prime Minister's Department.
"Who wants to complain? Sometimes the complaints go missing. Can you imagine complaining about the Chief Secretary to the Government, the Attorney-General, or a secretary-general? How can you?" she said.
Highlighting the rigid hierarchy within the civil service, Azalina said it is practically impossible for lower-ranking officers to report their superiors in the top Jusa C, B and A grades.
"For example, do you think a deputy director-general can complain about his boss? No way.
"They will block the complaint, or the paperwork might just go missing," she said, adding that malpractice is usually the reason such incidents occur.
She said the establishment of an independent ombudsman would force politicians and civil servants to act with integrity and ensure public grievances are addressed.
"If you have an ombudsman, people like me and all of us who work for the government will have to behave.
"Because when you know people can complain and there is no reason to hide the complaints, do you not think people will behave?" she said.
She warned that without true independence, the proposed ombudsman would be nothing more than a rebranded BPA and a "waste of the people's time".
"The confidence must be there for the people... If you do not allow people to complain, how do you know you are doing a good job?" she said.
Meanwhile, Legal Affairs Division (BHEUU) director-general Datuk Zamri Misman, who was also present, echoed the importance of public accountability, noting that the "name and shame" aspect of the ombudsman process is vital in driving institutional improvements.
On a related matter, Azalina said the proposed Freedom of Information (FOI) Act is equally vital to boost transparency in public administration and improve information delivery to the public.
She highlighted public frustration with bureaucratic red tape and unanswered queries.
"Can you imagine if you write a letter to a government department and, for months, it goes unanswered? This is why the FOI is necessary, because you have to answer the questions," she said.
Citing an everyday example, she said citizens currently face hurdles when seeking evidence to defend themselves in civil matters, such as traffic accidents, due to the Official Secrets Act (OSA).
"If you have an accident at a traffic light and the insurance company blames you, you might want to prove the traffic light malfunctioned.
"But when you go to the police station or the local council to ask for the traffic light footage or records, they refuse to respond, citing the OSA.
"You cannot pursue your dispute with the insurance company, which is a civil matter, because everybody who has the information hides behind the OSA," she said.
She said that under the FOI, the public will have the right to request such information to defend themselves in court.
"You have every right to ask the question, and you have every right to an answer, because you want to defend yourself.
"Under the FOI, the principle is that whatever information you want, we will provide it, except if it is highly sensitive, such as matters involving security. In such cases, the reasons will be explained to you," she said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Amendments to act will ensure Mara stays on course despite govt change — Zahid
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Proposed amendments to the Majlis Amanah Rakyat (Mara) Act are aimed at ensuring the agency's policies and governance remain consistent even when governments change, Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said today.
Speaking to reporters after closing the Musyawarah Nasional event today, Zahid said the amendments were necessary to prevent Mara from deviating from its founding objectives whenever there was a change in leadership or administration.
He said the proposed legal framework would ensure Mara continued operating according to its original mandate regardless of changes involving ministers, chairmen, senior management or the federal government itself.
"The act is being amended so that we return to the original foundation and ensure Mara' governance does not deviate whenever there is a change in director-general, chairman, minister or even government.
"The most saddening situation is when the government changes. The act must continue to be used not merely as guidance, but something that must and shall be adhered to," he said.
Zahid, who is also Rural and Regional Development Minister, said the amendments would institutionalise governance standards within Mara and strengthen accountability mechanisms across the agency.
He said he had already chaired two meetings involving Mara council members, senior officials and legal advisers to examine the proposed amendments in detail.
The deputy prime minister added that Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim had instructed Mara to prepare the amendments and a new legislative framework within three months.
However, Zahid said he had accelerated the process and intended to personally table the proposed amendments to the cabinet within the next two weeks.
"The prime minister instructed Mara to prepare the amendments and new act within three months. I responded by saying the amendments would be tabled to the cabinet within three weeks.
"One week has already gone by. God willing, within the next two weeks, I will table it myself," he said.
Zahid stressed that the amendments were not intended to alter Mara' core role but rather to reinforce the agency's original Bumiputera development agenda.
He traced Mara's origins to the Bumiputera Economic Congress organised by Umno in 1965, saying the institution must remain aligned with the principles behind its establishment.
"The establishment of Mara, which began as Rida, must continue on its original path," he said.
Mara was established in 1966 to spearhead Bumiputera socioeconomic development, particularly in education, entrepreneurship and rural economic advancement.
The proposed amendments come amid wider discussions on institutional reforms and governance continuity within government agencies following several changes in federal administrations over recent years.
Earlier, Zahid said resolutions emerging from the Musyawarah Nasional initiative would be coordinated across ministries, government agencies, government-linked companies (GLCs) and government-linked investment companies (GLICs) to ensure effective implementation.
He also described the Bumiputera and Islamic agenda discussed during the gathering as part of the national agenda anchored on constitutional provisions, including Article 153 of the Federal Constitution.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Lung cancer crisis worsening as 95pct cases detected too late, says Dzulkefly
Article:
CYBERJAYA: An estimated 95 per cent of lung cancer cases in Malaysia are detected only at Stage 3 or 4, contributing to a low five-year relative survival rate of about 11 per cent, according to Malaysian Council for Tobacco Control (MCTC).
Health Minister Datuk Seri Dr Dzulkefly Ahmad described the situation as a preventable epidemic that continues to claim lives and devastate families.
"Tobacco is the only legal product that kills half of its users when used exactly as intended. As a scientist by background, I understand all too well that there is no safe level of exposure to tobacco smoke," he said during the national-level World No Tobacco Day 2026 celebration and the National Tobacco Control Conference and Lung Health Summit 2026 today.
"There is no way the Health Ministry alone can manage a calamity of this scale, especially one that threatens the nation's future generations," he added.
Dzulkefly also officiated the launch of the LungShield Programme, a strategic collaboration involving IHH Healthcare, the Health Ministry and the police, aimed at strengthening early screening and diagnostic pathways to address the heavy burden of tobacco use.
Data from the Global Adult Tobacco Survey (GATS) Malaysia 2023 showed that 19.5 per cent of adults, or about 4.8 million people, currently use tobacco products, while 5.8 per cent, equivalent to 1.4 million adults, use electronic cigarettes. Both groups face heightened risks of chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD) and lung cancer.
Youth vaping also remains a major concern. The National Health and Morbidity Survey (NHMS) 2022 found that e-cigarette use among adolescents aged between 13 and 17 rose from 9.8 per cent in 2017 to 14.9 per cent in 2022.
In response, MCTC urged the government to reconsider reinstating the Generational End Game (GEG) provision under the Control of Smoking Products for Public Health Act (Act 852).
Dzulkefly said that the country could not afford to be complacent as nicotine addiction is increasingly being repackaged for a younger generation.
"We cannot allow addiction to be marketed as a lifestyle. We cannot allow harmful products to be made attractive to young people, and we cannot allow the health of future generations to be compromised in any way," he said.
MCTC chairman and National Cancer Society Malaysia managing director Professor Dr Murallitharan Munisamy described the GEG as a comprehensive strategy to protect future generations from nicotine addiction and reduce the long-term burden of lung disease.
"The high rate of late-stage cancer diagnoses in Malaysia is a sobering reminder that our intervention strategies must evolve. We must equip healthcare professionals with the tools and policy frameworks needed to break the cycle of nicotine addiction," he said.
Murallitharan added that the LungShield Programme reflected a shift towards earlier screening, faster referrals and better coordination through the use of technology and patient-centred support.
Meanwhile, University of Cyberjaya vice-chancellor Professor Dr David Whitford said tobacco control was also an educational responsibility for health sciences universities.
"Universities play an important role in strengthening research, shaping evidence-based policy discussions and preparing future healthcare professionals to address preventable diseases with scientific rigour and social responsibility," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MetMalaysia warns of thunderstorms across 10 states, including KL, until 4pm
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a warning of thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds affecting several states in Peninsular Malaysia and the Federal Territory of Kuala Lumpur until 4pm today.
In a statement, MetMalaysia said the adverse weather conditions are expected to affect Perlis, Kedah and Penang.
Also affected are Negeri Sembilan (Jelebu), Selangor (Hulu Selangor, Gombak, Petaling and Hulu Langat) as well as Johor (Segamat, Kluang, Kulai, Kota Tinggi and Johor Baru).
In addition, Pahang (Cameron Highlands, Lipis, Raub and Bentong), Perak (Kerian, Larut Matang and Selama, Hulu Perak, Kuala Kangsar, Kinta, Kampar, Batang Padang and Muallim) and Kelantan (Tumpat, Pasir Mas and Tanah Merah) are also involved.– BERNAMA
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: No hantavirus cases reported in Malaysia, surveillance stepped up
Article:
CYBERJAYA: The Health Ministry has ramped up surveillance and border controls to prevent a hantavirus outbreak, despite no local cases being reported among Malaysians as of May 10.
Health Minister Datuk Seri Dr Dzulkefly Ahmad said the ministry is maintaining a high level of preparedness through global monitoring and strict screening at all international entry points.
"No country is safe unless we all take a proactive approach to preparedness and stay on guard," he said at the World No Tobacco Day 2026 launch at the University of Cyberjaya.
He said that while six laboratory-confirmed cases have been reported elsewhere, these were linked to foreign vessels and did not involve the local population.
A key component of the ministry's four-point strategy involves the maritime sector, with rigorous health inspections conducted on all vessels entering Malaysian waters.
"It is crucial that we conduct health screenings on ships and vessels entering our waters, particularly those arriving from high-risk areas," Dzulkefly said.
As of end-May, a total of 22,367 ships and boats have been inspected at the country's gateways to ensure compliance with international health and sanitation standards.
The minister said the ministry has issued 2,421 pratique certificates and 1,146 ship sanitation certificates following these inspections.
"We want to ensure that every vessel entering the country complies with international public health and sanitation requirements," he added.
He said the Institute for Medical Research (IMR) is fully equipped with PCR testing capabilities to diagnose the virus, which is primarily transmitted by rodents.
"Our laboratory capacity at IMR is critical as it can carry out polymerase chain reaction (PCR) testing for diagnosis," he said.
He said the virus can lead to severe complications, including acute respiratory distress syndrome and haemorrhagic fever with renal syndrome.
He also stressed the importance of community empowerment and risk communication to keep the public informed about prevention.
"We must intensify public awareness campaigns so that people are more aware of hygiene practices and the importance of seeking early treatment if they have symptoms," he said.
He said that while the ministry does not want to be alarmist, it is essential that the health system remains responsive and alert.
"We do not want to alarm the public, but we also do not want people to think this only happens outside our borders. Our health system must stay at a high level of readiness," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Ayob Khan: Claims linking suspect's father to police are false
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: Deputy Inspector-General of Police Tan Sri Ayob Khan Mydin Pitchay said that claims linking a suspect's father in the college student murder case to police are false.
"I want to reiterate clearly that none of the suspects has any relation to the police or their families. That claim is untrue," he told newsmen here today.
Ayob's remarks come after Kelantan police dismissed viral claims that the father of a teenage suspect in the Ketereh murder case is a retired policeman with "
strong connections
".
District police chief Assistant Commissioner Mohd Azmir Damiri said the man is a retiree from another government agency, stressing that allegations of influence are untrue and urging the public not to believe unverified information.
Ayob said two elderly suspects in the case have been released, while two other suspects, both aged 19, have had their remand extended pending instructions from the Attorney-General's Chambers (A-GC).
The
murder of the 19-year-old student
attracted attention after she was stabbed 61 times and found in Kampung Padang Simah, Ketereh.
Ayob said police are waiting for instructions on the next course of action.
"Investigations must be completed before any decision is made," he said.
Ayob also urged the public and media to verify information before sharing or commenting on sensitive cases, warning that misinformation could fuel unnecessary tension.
"I urge not only the public but also the media to read and understand the full facts before drawing conclusions," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 'Others only talked, Anwar acted': Azalina commends PM for pursuing tenure limit law
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: While many past prime ministers have discussed limiting the tenure of the nation's top office, Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim is the only one who has taken concrete steps to make it a reality.
Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Law and Institutional Reform) Datuk Seri Azalina Othman Said said that Anwar should be commended for willingly subjecting his own tenure to such a limitation.
"To be very honest, if you were the prime minister, would you want to limit your power? No.
"That's why a lot of prime ministers have been talking about it but not many can do it.
"That's why the 10th prime minister, Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim, should be respected and commended because he is offering himself to be limited.
"If the limit is 10 years, he has already served for three years.
"Let's say the election is this year, he can become the prime minister for another seven years," she told law students at the closing ceremony of the Madani Accountability Framework townhall here, today.
Touching on the possibility of extending this framework to the state level, Azalina said that state governments have shown little interest in adopting similar term limits for their respective heads of government.
"At the states, there's one party that I won't mention, but they feel like if Allah wants you to be prime minister until you die, then you will be prime minister until you die.
"Some of them feel like power should not be limited. If possible, they should die in office, some of them think like that.
"But for me and the government of the day, we should limit it. And I think it's good.
"If you're good, you're good. Then, let the next person come in," she said.
However, the law failed to be passed as it did not reach the two-thirds majority needed for a constitutional amendment due to absent members of parliament.
The amendment is set to be re-tabled in parliament this June.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Musyawarah Nasional to be registered as an NGO - Zahid
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Musyawarah Nasional platform will be formally registered as a non-governmental organisation, with the Registrar of Societies (ROS) Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said today.
"The Permanent Secretariat of Musyawarah Nasional should take immediate action to register Musyawarah Nasional as a non-governmental organisation under the Registrar of Societies (ROS), Malaysia. It should not be established as a political coalition, but as an NGO that brings together the thinking of all Malays and Muslims in the country," he said today at the closing of the Musyawarah Nasional event titled "Malay Concerns: What Are the Solutions?
Zahid, who founded the platform said he wanted Musyawarah Nasional to become an organised, legitimate, credible and continuous platform. It cannot be a one-day, one-week, one-month or even one-year event.
He stressed that the Musyawarah Nasional does not belong to any individual, group or political party.
"It must belong to all who are sincere, who want to see this community stand with greater confidence, dignity and structure, and succeed in building its future in its own homeland," he said.
"It must be a long-term movement," he said at the conclusion of the Musyawarah Nasional session titled "Malay Concerns: What Is the Solution?" here today.
Ahmad Zahid said all presentations and proposals tabled during the session should also be consolidated into an official document as the first series of Musyawarah Nasional conclusions.
"Each core area should form an action committee to refine the proposals into a clearer implementation plan.
"Every two months, I hope the core chairpersons, together with their committees and appointed panels, will further deliberate on each paper presented today. Let this become a Malay, Bumiputera and Islamic agenda," he said.
He added that proposals involving ministries, agencies and government institutions should be structured as policy recommendations to be brought to the government.
"I myself will bring them forward. Tok Mat (Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan) will also bring them forward. All ministers representing us will do the same.
"It does not matter whether the minister is from Umno, PKR or Amanah—as long as we are Malays and Muslims, every minister has a responsibility to bring these matters to the decision-making table," he said.
"I will take responsibility not only to bring all these resolutions and views to the cabinet, but I will also coordinate with ministries, departments, agencies, government-linked companies (GLCs) and government-linked investment companies (GLICs), as well as relevant organisations for their implementation," he later told reporters.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] Govt to finalise proposed review of T20 fuel subsidies soon
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim says the proposal for more comprehensive targeted subsidies, particularly the rationalisation of RON95 fuel subsidies for the T20 income group, is still being refined and is expected to be finalised soon.
He said the proposal was currently being reviewed by the Crisis Management Task Force under the National Economic Action Council (NEAC), chaired by Tan Sri Mohd Hassan Marican.
"The proposal was tabled about a month ago by the task force chaired by Hassan. It has since been reviewed, and discussions were held on whether it should apply to the T5, T10, T15 or T20 income groups.
"In principle, we agree, but we want to ensure it does not affect the upper-middle class. They are working on it, and I hope it will be finalised soon," he told reporters on the sidelines of a dialogue session with Indian students at Universiti Malaya today.
Economists have previously estimated that excluding the T20 group from the Budi95 subsidy programme could save the government up to RM1.5 billion a month.
Yesterday, Umno Youth chief Datuk Dr Muhamad Akmal Saleh urged Putrajaya to implement a broader review of targeted subsidies, including the RON95 fuel subsidy for the T20 group. Akmal proposes redirecting fuel subsidies to
B40 and M40
groups.
He said savings from such rationalisation should instead be channelled towards the B40 and M40 groups, who are more affected by rising living costs, as part of efforts to strengthen the economy amid global uncertainty.
The proposal was among 25 resolutions discussed during the Rimbun 2.0 programme on economic sovereignty.
Currently, RON95 petrol is subsidised at RM1.99 per litre for Malaysians with a valid driving licence, subject to a monthly cap of 200 litres.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Sri Lanka to hike electricity tariff amid energy crisis
Article:
COLOMBO: Sri Lanka will increase electricity rates by up to 18 per cent from Monday to offset the additional costs of generating power using thermal plants due to the Middle East war, the Public Utilities Commission said.
Consumers using more than 180 units (kilowatt hours) of electricity a month will have to pay an additional 18 per cent from Monday, while those using less than that will not see their bills affected.
"The increase will apply to industries, hotels, businesses and government institutions and religious places of worship consuming more than 180 units a month," the commission said in a statement Sunday.
The measure is the latest in a series of steps taken by the island nation following the war in the Middle East.
The latest hike comes on top of a 40 per cent tariff increase introduced last month.
Sri Lanka has also raised fuel prices by more than 35 per cent and rationed the same following energy supply disruptions.
Higher energy prices have pushed inflation to more than double, reaching 5.4 per cent in April, according to official data.
Sri Lanka has been slowly emerging from the 2022 economic meltdown, when it ran out of foreign exchange reserves to pay for essential imports such as food, fuel and medicines.
It was hit hard by a cyclone last year that killed at least 643 people and affected more than 10 per cent of the island's population of 22 million.
The storm caused an estimated US$4.1 billion in direct physical damage to buildings and agriculture, according to the World Bank.
The country has been stabilising its fragile economy with the help of a US$2.9 billion IMF bailout agreed in early 2023, but high energy prices have posed a serious challenge to recovery efforts.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Zahid proposes mandatory full-time halal executives for companies holding Jakim certification
Article:
KUAL LUMPUR: All companies holding halal certification from the Department of Islamic Development Malaysia (Jakim) should be required to appoint a full-time halal executive to ensure strict compliance with halal standards, Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said today.
Zahid, who is also chairman of the National Halal Council, said the proposal was aimed at strengthening preventive mechanisms rather than relying solely on enforcement after breaches occur.
Speaking at the closing session of the Musyawarah Nasional titled "Malay Concerns: What Are the Solutions?, he said the current approach of revoking halal certification only after violations is no longer sufficient.
"In halal matters, we cannot act only after a breach has occurred. Revoking certificates after problems arise is not enough. We need a more proactive and responsible prevention system," he said.
"I am confident those in this room will agree with my suggestion that every company holding a Jakim halal certificate should be required to have a full-time halal executive in a permanent position," he said.
Zahid also called for the establishment of a halal auditor function to scrutinise advertising, packaging, menus, digital platforms and marketing materials.
He said this was necessary to address the increasing use of ambiguous terms such as "Muslim-friendly", "no pork, no lard" and "Muslim choice" in the market.
"We do not want these terms to confuse consumers. What we want is a clear halal certification mark if a product is truly halal, so that Muslims are not misled by these labels," he said.
Zahid also highlighted that 67 per cent of halal products in the market are produced by non-Muslim entrepreneurs, stressing the need for better integration and oversight within the industry.
"Islamic products are not only for Muslims. They are for all religions. Halalan tayyiban is scientifically verified, and its cleanliness is proven. We must dispel the misconception that halal is only for Muslims," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Kuching mayor: Public anger understandable, but illegal to dump carcass of dog that attacked jogger into river
Article:
KUCHING, May 10 — The disposal of the carcass of a dog reported to have attacked a woman while she was jogging at a park in Stutong last Friday into a river is an offence under local cleanliness by-laws, said Kuching South Mayor Dato Wee Hong Seng.
Wee said although public anger over the incident was understandable, disposing of the carcass into a river remained against the law.
“If you dispose of things into the river or elsewhere, of course it is against the law because that is not the proper thing to do. It falls under the cleanliness by-law.
“You cannot simply throw anything into a river or any place you like,” he told reporters when asked during the Kuching South City Council (MBKS) Community Market and Hawker Reunion Luncheon at Stutong Market here.
Wee said officers from MBKS and the Department of Veterinary Services Sarawak (DVSS), who went to the scene after the incident, were informed that the dog had already been beaten to death by members of the public before its carcass was disposed of in a nearby river.
“What they discovered was that the dog had been killed by members of the public and thrown into the river,” he said.
He said authorities were unable to determine whether the dog had rabies as the carcass could not be recovered for testing.
“Whether the dog had rabies or not, we do not know. But it would not be fair to simply say it did not have rabies because a normal dog, especially towards people who had been feeding it, should not behave in such a manner,” he said.
A video circulating on social media showed a woman being attacked by a black dog while jogging at the park, while another man attempted to stop the attack.
According to Wee, preliminary information suggested the victim had allegedly been feeding the dog prior to the incident.
He added that the dog’s aggressive behaviour was unusual, particularly towards someone familiar to it.
“A normal dog would not behave like that, especially towards the person feeding it,” he said.
Wee, however, stressed that his primary concern was the wellbeing of those who handled or killed the dog during the incident, urging them to seek immediate medical attention and anti-rabies vaccination as a precautionary measure.
“We are more concerned about those who handled the dog. We hope they will come forward to get checked and vaccinated because they may not realise if they had been scratched or exposed to saliva,” he said.
He added that MBKS would not take action against members of the public who intervened to rescue the victim.
“I think anyone would have reacted the same way in such a situation because the priority was to save the victim. That is a natural reaction,” he said.
Wee also reminded pet owners to act responsibly and ensure their pets were properly confined.
“If you are a pet owner, make sure your pet is kept within your compound and not allowed to roam freely,” he said.
He said MBKS and DVSS would continue efforts to manage stray dogs and contain rabies cases in the city, including vaccination programmes for stray animals.
“We are doing our best, but we also hope the public will play their part and be more understanding,” he said. — The Borneo Post

Title: Sabah business groups back say bringing back barter trade could boost local economies
Article:
KOTA KINABALU, May 10 — Several of Sabah’s leading trade and maritime associations have voiced strong support for Chief Minister Datuk Seri Panglima Hajiji Noor’s proposal to revive barter trade along the state’s eastern maritime borders and northern Kudat region.
In a joint statement, the Sabah Shipping Agents Association (SSAA), Sabah Freight Forwarders and Logistics Association (SABFFLA), ASEAN-China Commerce Association (ACCA) and Kadazandusun Chamber of Commerce and Industry (KCCI) described the move as a strategic initiative aligned with the Sabah Maju Jaya (SMJ) 2.0 roadmap and the state’s Blue Economy agenda.
While expressing support for reviving such activities through the Sandakan, Tawau and Lahad Datu hubs, SABFFLA said that although barter trade volumes are smaller compared to containerised cargo, the sector remains an important source of income for logistics operators and local service providers.
The association also welcomed the Chief Minister’s announcement regarding the Lahad Datu Customs, Immigration, Quarantine and Security (CIQS) complex for passengers, saying the inclusion of barter trade activities would further maximise the facility’s potential.
Meanwhile, ACCA president Datuk Lim Shau Hui said the suspension of barter trade between Kudat and Palawan in the southern Philippines had contributed to economic stagnation in the district.
He urged the government to expedite the revival process while ensuring excessive bureaucracy and administrative hurdles do not delay implementation.
SSAA president Chow Yang Ann said traders from the southern Philippines relied heavily on Sabah’s port infrastructure, pointing to the steady flow of containerised cargo between Sandakan and Zamboanga as evidence of strong regional demand.
“Formalising and regulating barter trade will further optimise our port facilities,” he said.
KCCI Trade and Investment representative Carl V Moosom, who previously led the BIMP-EAGA Business Council Sabah, said reopening regulated barter trade routes would generate positive economic spillover effects for local districts.
“It is economically logical for communities in the Tawi-Tawi archipelago and Palawan to source goods from Sabah rather than the more distant Philippine mainland,” he said.
The coalition added that the initiative would strengthen Sabah’s position as a regional transshipment and trade hub within the BIMP-EAGA region by leveraging the state’s strategic geographical location and integrating barter trade activities with the new CIQS facilities. — The Borneo Post

Title: AADK detects over 1,000 drug-positive cases at Malaysia border entry points since 2024
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, May 10 — The National Anti-Drug Agency (AADK) recorded 1,084 positive drug cases from inspections at the country’s border entry points for the period 2024 to March 31 this year.
AADK director-general Datuk Ruslin Jusoh said the number was obtained from screening of 3,625 Malaysians returning from neighbouring countries, especially during the festive season and school holidays.
He said in 2024, a total of 494 individuals were arrested after testing positive for drugs involving 444 men and 50 women with methamphetamine recording the highest number of cases at 148 cases, followed by ketamine (109 cases), tetrahydrocannabinol (THC) or cannabis (96 cases), ‘4-methylenedioxymethamphetamine’ (MDMA) (80 cases), benzo (62 cases), ketum (23 cases) and opiates (four cases).
“Last year, a total of 415 people were arrested, involving 392 men and 23 women, with the highest drug recorded being methamphetamine in 140 cases, followed by ketamine (108 cases), THC (70 cases), benzo (59 cases), MDMA (31 cases), opiates (five cases) and ketum in two cases.
“A total of 175 individuals aged 18 to 60 were arrested this year until March 31, involving 167 men and eight women, with the highest drug recorded being methamphetamine in 65 cases, followed by ketamine (43 cases), THC (32 cases), MDMA (22 cases) and ketum (13 cases),” he told reporters after the 2026 Drug-Free Aspiration Tour: Gombak-Kita Turun Padang Aspiration Tour in Kampung Kemensah, Ulu Klang, here, today.
Ruslin also reminded Malaysians not to involved in drug abuse while abroad because strict action can be taken under the Drug Addicts Act 1983.
According to him, AADK has placed Border Anti-Drug Units in Kedah, Perlis and Kelantan to monitor the entry of Malaysians from neighbouring countries.
“All the units are placed in the relevant locations to monitor entry movements and carry out inspections including at the ferry route in Pengkalan Kubor, Kelantan,” he said.
Meanwhile, Ruslin said, the 2026 Drug-Free Aspiration Tour (JABD) Programme in Kampung Kemensah, Ulu Klang also involves the Kampung Angkat Bebas Dadah (KABD) initiative.
He said KABD is a national community-based initiative to raise awareness about the dangers of drugs and promote a healthy lifestyle. — Bernama

Title: New Zealand tourist breaks leg after being hit by motorcycle in Chow Kit jaywalking incident
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, May 10 — A New Zealand tourist suffered a broken leg after he was hit by a motorcycle while allegedly jaywalking along Chow Kit Road heading towards Sogo here early this morning.
Kuala Lumpur Traffic Investigation and Enforcement Department (JSPT) chief ACP Mohd Zamzuri Mohd Isa said police received a report on the accident at about 2.37 am, involving a Yamaha Y15ZR motorcycle ridden by a 26-year-old local man and a 49-year-old male tourist from New Zealand.
“As a result of the accident, the motorcyclist suffered minor injuries to his hands and legs, while the pedestrian sustained a fractured right leg, injuries to the back of his head and swelling on his right hand, and is receiving treatment at Kuala Lumpur Hospital,” he said in a statement today.
He said preliminary investigations indicated that the pedestrian had suddenly crossed the road at a location that was not designated as a pedestrian crossing, causing the collision with the motorcycle.
According to him, both individuals involved had been identified, with the tourist still receiving treatment at Kuala Lumpur Hospital, while the motorcyclist had been called in to provide his statement.
“Kuala Lumpur JSPT had also reviewed closed-circuit television (CCTV) recordings, visited the scene and recorded statements from those involved as part of the investigation,” he said, adding that the case is being investigated under Section 43(1) of the Road Transport Act 1987. — Bernama

Title: Kommuniti Madani must be govt’s eyes and ears at grassroots level, says Fahmi
Article:
